Revision                                                                                                                             Local extrema for a function of one variable
                                                                                                                                       Assume that f : [a, b] → R is continous and that f ′ and f ′′
                                                                                                                                       exist are continuous on (a, b). To find the local maximum
                                                                                                                                       points, local minimum points and points of inflection of f , we
    I A scalar field is a function that assigns to every
                                                                                                                                       proceed as follows.
      point (x, y) in the plane (2D space) or point (x, y, z) in
      space (3D space) a scalar (number) value f (x, y)                                                                                  1. Find every point x0 ∈ (a, b) such that f ′ (x0 ) = 0.
      or f (x, y, z), respectively.
                                                                                                                                         2. Evaluate f ′′ (x0 ).
    I An example would be the surface temperature at every                                                                                     I If f ′′ (x0 ) < 0, then x0 is a local maximum point;
      point in Australia. Another example is the pressure                                                                                      I If f ′′ (x0 ) > 0, then x0 is a local minimum point;
                                                                                                                                               I If f ′′ (x0 ) = 0, and f ′′′ (x0 ) ̸= 0 then x0 is a point of
      distribution in a fluid.
                                                                                                                                                 inflection.

                                                                                                                                         3. Separately consider the endpoints a and b (that is, the
                                                                                                                                            boundary points of the interval [a, b]).

                                                                                                                                                                          g(x, y) = 0. We then look for minimum and maximum
                                                                                                                                                                          values of f (x, y) for all (x, y) such that g(x, y) = 0.

                                                                                                                             Example 9
                                                                                                                             Find the dimensions of the closed circular cylinder of smallest
                                                                                                                             surface area whose volume is 16π cm3 . Recall that the surface
                                                                                                                             area S and volume V of a cylinder with radius r and height h
                                                                                                                             are
                                                                                                                                          S = 2πr2 + 2πrh and V = πr2 h.
                                                                                                                             Solution: We have to find r and h that minimise S(r, h),
                                                                                                                             subject to the constraint V (r, h) = 16π. Also, only values
                                                                                                                             r > 0 and h > 0 make geometric sense.
                                                                                                                             Introduce the Lagrangian function
                                                                                                                                          L(r, h, λ) = S(r, h) − λ(V (r, h) − 16π)

Example 10
Use Lagrange multipliers to find the distance between the
plane 3x + 4y + 12z = 6 and the point (5, 7, 11). How do we
know that this is the minimum distance and not a maximum?

                                                                                                                                  Gradient and Directional Derivatives


                                                                                                                                   The temperature at every point in the room is a scalar field.
                                                                                                                                   Consider a point P = (x0 , y0 , z0 ) in the room. This section
                                                                                                                                   helps answer the following questions.
                                                                                                                                     I What is the direction and magnitude of maximum
                                                                                                                                        increase of the temperature from the point P ?
                                                                                                                                     I What is the direction and magnitude of maximum
                                                                                                                                        decrease of the temperature from the point P ?
                                                                                                                                     I What is the rate of change of the temperature in a
                                                                                                                                        direction ⃗b from the point P ?
  Shortest distance of 13 at the point (2, 3, −1) on the plane

 Gradient of a scalar field                                                                                                         Example 12
                                                                                                                                   Let ϕ(x, y, z) = x2 y 3 z + 2y be a scalar field. Calculate
   Definition 11                                                                                                                   grad(ϕ).
   The gradient of ϕ is the vector field defined by
                                           (                                       )T
                  ∂ϕ ⃗ ∂ϕ ⃗ ∂ϕ ⃗               ∂ϕ ∂ϕ ∂ϕ
        grad(ϕ) =    i+    j+    k=              ,  ,                                        .
                  ∂x    ∂y    ∂z               ∂x ∂y ∂z

   is the formal vector field
